teachings.text: I AM the bread of life — whoever comes to me shall not hunger; whoever believes shall not thirst
I AM the light of the world — whoever follows me will not walk in darkness
I AM the door / gate — whoever enters through me will be saved
I AM the good shepherd — I know my sheep and lay down my life for them
I AM the resurrection and the life — whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live
I AM the way, the truth, and the life — no one comes to the Father except through me
I AM the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser
Before Abraham was, I AM
I AM the Alpha and the Omega, the root and offspring of David, the bright morning star
Warns that those who do not believe he is who he claims will die in their sins
Contrasts the true shepherd who enters by the door and is known by his sheep with the thief who climbs in another way
All who came before him are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them
The hireling, who is not the shepherd, sees the wolf coming and flees because he does not own the sheep, leaving them to be caught and scattered
Promises to lay down his life for the sheep and to gather other sheep not of this fold into one flock under one shepherd
I and the Father are one — the Father is in me and I am in the Father
He who has seen me has seen the Father — I do nothing on my own but speak as the Father taught me
The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hand — all things have been delivered to me; no one knows the Son except the Father
The Father is greater than I; those who honor the Son honor the Father who sent him
As a boy in the temple, asks his parents why they searched — he must be about his Father's business
He defends his Sabbath healing by declaring that his Father works until now and he himself works
Teaches that the Son acts only in concert with the Father, who loves the Son and shows him all his works
Names his witnesses — John the Baptist, his own works, and the Father — to confirm his judgment is just
His teaching is not his own but the Father's — anyone willing to do God's will can recognize it as divine
Tells them they judge by the flesh while he judges no one — yet his judgment is true because the Father is with him
He tells them they neither know him nor his Father; if they had known him, they would have known the Father also
His honor comes from the Father — they claim God but do not know him, while the Son knows him and keeps his word
He asks for which of the many good works shown them from the Father do they seek to stone him
Cites Psalm 82 to defend his claim — if Scripture calls others 'gods,' why call it blasphemy when the Son of God claims it?
To see and believe in the Son is to see and believe in the Father who sent him — he came as light to save, not to judge
Affirms his words come from the Father, whose commandment is everlasting life
Tells the disciples that to know him is to know the Father — and from now on they have seen the Father
The Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins and is Lord of the Sabbath
All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me
Defines his mission — to fulfill the Law, call sinners to repentance, seek the lost, and give his life as ransom
He came to cast fire on the earth — not peace but a sword
Counters the chief priests' challenge with a question — the baptism of John, whence was it, from heaven or of men?
Refuses to answer when they would not commit — neither tell I you by what authority I do these things
Reminds the Pharisees that hungry David ate the showbread reserved for priests
Calls a man with a withered hand forward and asks whether it is lawful to do good or save life on the Sabbath
He tells his mother at Cana that his hour has not yet come
He tells the disciples he has meat to eat that they know nothing of
Tells the disciples the man's blindness is not from sin but exists so God's works can be revealed in him
Tells the disciples Lazarus's sickness will not end in death but will glorify God and the Son of God
Tells the disciples Lazarus has fallen asleep and he is going to wake him — those who walk by day do not stumble
He assures Martha that her brother shall rise again.
He descended from heaven and is from above — not of this world; before Abraham was, I AM
He came from the Father into the world and now returns to the Father
Came from heaven to do the Father's will — to lose none given to him but raise them up at the last day
He tells the crowd not to murmur among themselves
He asks the murmuring disciples whether his teaching offends them, and what if they should see the Son of Man ascend up where he was before
Tells his brothers his time has not come — the world hates him because he testifies its works are evil
He cries in the temple that he is not come of himself but is from him who sent him; he knows the Father, but they do not
Foretells he will soon return to the Father — and they cannot follow where he goes

subcategories.title: Predictions of His Suffering, Death, and Resurrection

teachings.text: The Son of Man must suffer many things, be rejected, be killed, and rise on the third day
As Jonah was in the fish three days, so will the Son of Man be in the earth; destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it
As Moses lifted up the serpent, so must the Son of Man be lifted up — and when lifted, will draw all people
The good shepherd lays down his life of his own accord; unless a grain of wheat falls and dies, it cannot bear fruit
Everything written about him in the Prophets will be accomplished; the Christ must suffer and rise on the third day
Rebukes Peter for opposing the cross — 'Get behind me, Satan' — Peter's mind is on man's things, not God's
Charges Peter, James, and John to keep the Transfiguration secret until after his resurrection
Affirms Elijah has already come and was mistreated — and the Son of Man will suffer the same way
Rebukes Peter for opposing his foretold suffering — 'Get behind me, Satan'
Speaks of the baptism of suffering ahead and how distressed he is until it is accomplished
Foretells he will be handed to the Gentiles, mocked, scourged, killed — and rise on the third day
He drives out the dove sellers from the temple, declaring that his Father's house must not be made a house of merchandise
He defends Mary's anointing — she has kept this for the day of his burying; the poor are always with you, but he will not always be present.
Announces that the hour for the Son of Man to be glorified has come
Warns them in advance so they will believe — the prince of this world comes but has no claim on him
He declares to the Samaritan woman: I who speak to you am the Messiah
The works done in the Father's name bear witness — if not doing the Father's works, do not believe
The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the dead are raised, the poor have good news — blessed is he who does not stumble over me
Identifies John the Baptist as the prophesied Elijah for those willing to accept it
Defines his earthly mission — sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel
Asks the disciples at Caesarea Philippi — whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
Presses the disciples to confess for themselves who they say he is
Blesses Peter for his confession — flesh and blood did not reveal it to him, but the Father in heaven
Reassures the disciples after the Transfiguration — arise, and be not afraid
Teaches Peter that the sons are free from the temple tax, but provides it from a coin in a fish's mouth to avoid offense
Declares his mission to seek the lost — for the Son of man is come to save that which was lost
Sends two disciples to fetch the colt for the triumphal entry — tell the owner the Lord has need of it
Silences the Pharisees with a question about Christ — whose son is he? If David in spirit calls him Lord, how is he his son?
Affirms that Elijah comes first to restore all things — and has already come and been mistreated
Defends the disciples' praise at the triumphal entry — if they were silent, even the stones would cry out
He greets Nathanael as a true Israelite without guile and reveals he saw him under the fig tree before Philip called
Promises Nathanael will see greater things — heaven open and angels ascending and descending on the Son of Man
He asks the Samaritan woman at the well to give him a drink
He tells the woman to call her husband and reveals he knows she has had five husbands and the man she now has is not her husband
He reveals himself to the healed man, declaring that the one who talks with him is the Son of God
Announces the Son of Man is now glorified and warns the disciples he will be with them only a little while longer
Defends his disciples by citing David eating the showbread and priests working on the Sabbath — one greater than the temple is here
This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears
Cleanses a leper with a touch and instructs him to show himself to the priest and offer the gift Moses commanded as a testimony
Responds to the centurion's request for his servant — I will come and heal him
Authorizes demons to enter the herd of swine with a single word — Go
Silences the mourners at Jairus's house declaring the girl is not dead but sleeping, then raises her with the words 'Talitha cumi'
Charges the healed blind men to silence — see that no man know it
Heals a man's withered hand on the Sabbath
Calls for the loaves and fishes before feeding the five thousand — bring them hither to me
Asks the disciples how many loaves they have before feeding the four thousand
Rebukes an unclean spirit in the Capernaum synagogue, commanding it to be silent and come out
Peace, be still — Jesus rebukes the wind and commands the sea, and there was a great calm
Confronts the Gerasene demoniac, asking the unclean spirit its name — Legion
Perceives that virtue has gone out of him and asks who touched him in the crowd
Tells the disciples to feed the crowd themselves and asks how many loaves they have — sets up the feeding of five thousand
Ephphatha — Be opened — Jesus heals the deaf man with an impeded speech impediment
Has compassion on the crowd that has gone three days without food and resolves to feed them before sending them home
Casts out a deaf and mute spirit from a boy and forbids it to return
Asks blind Bartimaeus what he wants and restores his sight, declaring his faith has made him whole
Raises the widow's son at Nain from his bier with a word
Turns water into wine at Cana — instructs the servants to fill the jars and serve the master of the feast
He sends the nobleman home with the word that his son lives, healing the boy at a distance
He commands the lame man to rise, take up his bed, and walk
He asks Philip where they shall buy bread to feed the great multitude that has come to him
He instructs the disciples to make the men sit down before the feeding of the five thousand
He tells the disciples to gather up the leftover fragments so that nothing be lost
He sends the man born blind to wash in the pool of Siloam, and the man returns seeing
At Lazarus's tomb, commands the stone removed, thanks the Father, and calls Lazarus out
Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand — the time is fulfilled
He has come to call sinners to repentance, not the righteous; unless you repent you will all likewise perish
There is more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than ninety-nine who need no repentance
Parable of the barren fig tree — the owner gives one final year to bear fruit before judgment falls
Pronounces woe on Chorazin and Bethsaida — Tyre and Sidon would have repented in sackcloth and ashes had they seen such mighty works
Asks whether the Galileans Pilate killed were greater sinners — calls all to repent or likewise perish
Asks whether the eighteen killed when the tower in Siloam fell were greater sinners than other Jerusalemites
Calls Zacchaeus down from the sycamore tree — he must stay at his house today
Declares salvation has come to Zacchaeus's house — he too is a son of Abraham
He finds the healed man in the temple and warns him to sin no more lest a worse thing come upon him
Lost sheep — a shepherd leaves ninety-nine to search for the one; rejoices when found
Lost coin — a woman searches the whole house; calls her neighbors when found
Lost son — a son returns from ruin; his father runs to meet him and restores him fully
Come to me, all who are weary and burdened — take my yoke and learn from me; I am gentle and lowly; my yoke is easy and burden light
Promises that all whom the Father gives him will come to him, and whoever comes he will never cast out
He is the true vine, the Father is the vinedresser — every fruitless branch is removed; every fruitful branch is pruned to bear more
Abide in him as the branch in the vine — apart from him you can do nothing; whoever does not abide withers and is burned
Promises that abiding disciples can ask what they wish — the Father is glorified when they bear much fruit
As the Father has loved him, so has he loved them — abide in his love by keeping his commandments, as he kept the Father's
These things he has spoken so that his joy may be in them and their joy may be full
Promises that those who love him and keep his word will be loved by the Father — and the Father and Son will dwell with them
They are already clean through the word he has spoken to them.

subcategories.title: Prayer for Himself — The Hour Has Come

teachings.text: Father, the hour has come — glorify your Son that he may glorify you; he has authority to give eternal life to all you have given him
Asks the Father to glorify him with the glory they shared before the world existed
He asks the Father to restore the glory they shared before the world existed
Defines eternal life — knowing the only true God and Jesus Christ whom he sent
Prays for those the Father has given him — that they be kept united and protected from the evil one
Sanctify them in the truth — your word is truth; as the Father sent him into the world, he sends them; for their sake he consecrates himself
Acknowledges the disciples have received his words and believed the Father sent him
All things that are his are the Father's, and all that are the Father's are his — and he is glorified in his disciples.
Tells the Father he kept all but the son of perdition — now he prays the disciples may have his joy though the world hates them
Tells the Father his disciples do not belong to the world, just as he does not
Prays for all future believers — that their unity may mirror the Father and Son and testify the Father sent him
Asks that the disciples be with him to see his glory — and that the Father's love would dwell in them
O righteous Father, the world has not known you, but he has known you, and these have known that you sent him.
He prays that they all may be one as the Father and Son are one — that the world may believe that the Father sent him
Names the greatest commandment — love God wholly — with love of neighbor as second, summing up the Law and Prophets
Love your neighbor as yourself — the second commandment, like the first
The Good Samaritan — a despised outsider shows mercy to the man left for dead; go and do likewise
Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you, pray for those who persecute you — be merciful as the Father is merciful
If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Even tax collectors do the same; lend expecting nothing in return
Do to others as you would have them do to you — this sums up the Law and the Prophets
A new commandment: love one another as I have loved you — by this all will know you are his disciples
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down his life for his friends; you are his friends if you do what he commands
He no longer calls them servants but friends — he has made known everything he heard from the Father
If you love me, keep my commandments.
You are the salt of the earth — if it loses its saltiness it is worthless
You are the light of the world — a city on a hill cannot be hidden; let your light shine so others may glorify the Father
Submits to John's baptism — it is fitting to fulfill all righteousness
He has not come to abolish the Law but to fulfill it — not one jot or tittle will pass; whoever relaxes even the least commandment will be least in the kingdom
Sets the standard for kingdom entry — righteousness that surpasses the scribes and Pharisees

subcategories.title: The Antitheses — “You Have Heard… But I Say to You”

teachings.text: Anger: murder begins in the heart — contempt and insult bring one into judgment
Lust: adultery begins with a lustful look; if eye or hand causes sin, remove it
Divorce: a certificate of divorce causes adultery, except for sexual immorality
Oaths: do not swear at all — let your yes be yes and your no be no; anything more is from evil
Retaliation: do not resist the evil one — turn the other cheek, give your cloak, go the extra mile
Enemies: love, pray for, and do good to enemies — be perfect as the Father is perfect
Teaches the tree is known by its fruit — the mouth speaks from the abundance of the heart
Give in secret, not before others to be seen — your Father who sees in secret will reward you
Fast without showing it — anoint your head, wash your face; true piety is seen by God alone
Do not judge, or you will be judged — first remove the log from your own eye, then help your brother with his
Do not give what is holy to dogs or pearls to pigs; judge with righteous judgment, not by appearances

subcategories.title: The Golden Rule and Two Ways

teachings.text: Whatever you wish others would do to you, do also to them — this is the Law and the Prophets
He who hears his words and does them is like a wise man who built on rock; who hears but does not act built on sand

subcategories.title: The Nature of Greatness in the Kingdom

teachings.text: Whoever would be great must be a servant — whoever would be first must be slave of all; the last will be first
Everyone who exalts himself will be humbled; whoever humbles himself will be exalted; the greatest is the servant of all
Whoever receives a child in his name receives him — the least among all is the greatest
Reverses worldly ranks in the kingdom — but many that are first shall be last, and the last shall be first
Exposes the disciples' road argument over which of them is greatest
Asks James and John what they want before answering their request for the seats of honor
The Son of Man came not to be served but to serve and give his life as a ransom for many
After washing the disciples' feet, calls them to do likewise — the servant is not greater than the master
What he is doing Peter does not now understand but will know later — and unless Peter is washed, he has no part with Jesus.
Tells Peter the bathed person needs only their feet washed — and the disciples are clean, though not all
He asks if they understand what he has done; they rightly call him Master and Lord, for so he is.
Do not take the seat of honor — sit in the lowest place; when you give a banquet, invite those who cannot repay you
The Pharisee and the tax collector — self-righteousness condemned; humility is justified
Teaches that disciples obeying every command are still unworthy servants — duty earns no praise
Calls disciples to become like little children — only the humble enter the kingdom and are greatest in it
Defends the children's praise in the temple by quoting Psalm 8 — God ordains praise from the mouths of infants

subcategories.title: The Power and Accountability of Words

teachings.text: People will give account for every careless word on judgment day — by your words you will be justified or condemned
What comes out of the mouth proceeds from the heart — this is what defiles a person
Do not take oaths at all — not by heaven, earth, Jerusalem, or your own head; let your yes be yes and your no be no
Declares himself the way, the truth, and the life — no one comes to the Father except through him
His testimony is valid — he knows where he came from; two witnesses confirm truth: himself and the Father
You will know the truth and the truth will set you free; if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed
He who has ears to hear, let him hear — a call to spiritual attention repeated throughout his ministry
Tells the disciples kingdom mysteries are given to them — others see and hear without perceiving
Pay attention to what you hear — to the one who has, more will be given; from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken
Summons the multitude to attend his teaching on defilement — hear, and understand
Rebukes the disciples' dullness — what enters the mouth simply passes through, but what comes out defiles
If any man have ears to hear, let him hear — Jesus presses the multitude to receive the teaching
The Pharisees are blind guides of the blind — both fall into a pit; you can read the sky but not the signs of the times
Because you say “I see” — your guilt remains; you search the Scriptures thinking they give life, yet refuse to come to him
Rebukes the Pharisees and Sadducees — they read the weather but cannot discern the signs of the times
Refuses the Pharisees' demand for a sign — no sign will be given to this generation
Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod — guard your hearts from their teaching
Rebukes the disciples for missing the bread analogy — they have eyes and ears yet still do not understand
Tells the crowd they predict the weather expertly but cannot discern the present time
Declares his coming inverts sight — the unseeing receive sight, the self-confident are made blind
Beware of false prophets in sheep's clothing — you will know them by their fruits; a good tree cannot bear bad fruit
Beware of false christs and false prophets who perform great signs to deceive, if possible, even the elect
Warns the disciples to guard against the hypocrisy of the Pharisees
Warns that an unclean spirit returning with seven worse spirits leaves the person's final state worse than the first
Names the verdict — light came into the world but people loved darkness because their deeds were evil
Nothing is covered that will not be uncovered — what is whispered in private will be proclaimed on the housetops
A lamp is not brought in to be hidden — nothing is secret except to come to light
Your sins are forgiven — the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins
The one forgiven much loves much — the one forgiven little loves little
Warns that those who refuse to forgive will not be forgiven by the Father
Two debtors forgiven by a creditor — the one forgiven most loves most; this woman has loved much, while Simon offered no water, kiss, or oil
Forgive and you will be forgiven — if you do not forgive others, your Father will not forgive you
Forgive not seven times but seventy-seven / seventy times seven — without limit
The unforgiving servant — forgiven a vast debt, he refuses to forgive a small one
Leave your gift at the altar — first be reconciled to your brother, then come and offer it
If your brother sins against you, go privately — if he listens, you have won him; take witnesses; then tell the church
Settle matters quickly with your adversary while on the way
He tells the accusers of the woman caught in adultery that he who is without sin among them should cast the first stone
He asks the woman where her accusers are and tells her that neither does he condemn her — go and sin no more
From the beginning God made them male and female — what God has joined, let no one separate; Moses permitted divorce only because of hardness of heart
Whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another commits adultery
In the resurrection, people neither marry nor are given in marriage — they are like angels
Teaches that some forgo marriage for the kingdom — those able to accept this calling should do so
Whoever looks with lustful intent has already committed adultery in his heart; if eye or hand causes sin, remove it
What comes out of a person defiles him — from within come sexual immorality, adultery, and licentiousness
Honor your father and mother; yet whoever loves family more than Jesus is not worthy of him
He came not to bring peace but division — households will be divided; whoever does the will of God is his true family
Those who leave family or possessions for the kingdom will receive many times more, with eternal life
Who is my mother, or my brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will are my true family
Identifies his true family as those who hear and obey the word of God
Let the little children come — do not hinder them; to such belongs the kingdom of heaven
Whoever causes one of these little ones to stumble — better a millstone around his neck; do not despise them
Tells the disciples not to stop the unknown exorcist — whoever is not against them is for them
Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men — come, follow me
Foxes have holes and birds have nests — the Son of Man has no place to lay his head; let the dead bury their own dead
No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom
Tells the anxious Martha that Mary has chosen the one needful thing, and it will not be taken from her
Calls Matthew the tax collector from his booth — Follow me
Redefines family — who is my mother and brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will
Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the tax collector to leave his post and become a disciple
Calls the returning apostles to withdraw with him to a quiet place and rest
Reads Isaiah 61 in the Nazareth synagogue — anointed to preach to the poor and proclaim the Lord's favor
Tells Peter to put out into deep water and let down the nets for the miraculous catch
Calls Peter from his nets — from now on he will catch people
Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the publican from the receipt of custom
He asks the first disciples what they seek and invites them to come and see where he dwells
He renames Simon son of Jonah as Cephas, meaning a stone, marking him with a new identity
He reminds the twelve that he has chosen them, yet one of them is a devil
He calls the disciples to go back into Judaea again despite the danger awaiting him there.
Where he goes Peter cannot follow now, but shall follow afterwards.
Anyone who would follow must deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow — the cost is total self-surrender
Whoever saves his life will lose it — whoever loses it for his sake will find it; what profits a man to gain the world and forfeit his soul?
Anyone who does not renounce family, possessions, and even his own life cannot be a disciple
The tower builder and the king going to war — count the cost before you begin
Tells the disciples not to send the crowd away — they themselves should feed them
Confronts shallow disciples — why call him Lord but ignore his commands?
Stops the disciples from forbidding the unaffiliated exorcist working in his name
Rebukes James and John for wanting to call fire on the Samaritans — he came to save lives, not destroy them
He asks the twelve whether they also will go away
Defines true discipleship as continuing in his word
A good tree bears good fruit; a bad tree cannot — by their fruits you shall know them; every fruitless tree is cut down
He who abides in him bears much fruit — apart from him you can do nothing; the Father is glorified by much fruit
He chose them to go and bear fruit that abides; if his words abide in them, they may ask whatever they wish
Sends two disciples to fetch a never-ridden colt — say the Lord has need of him if questioned
Teaches that those who lose their life for him keep it — and even with a troubled soul, he chose this hour to glorify the Father
Promises that those who endure to the end will be saved
On this rock he will build his church — the gates of hell shall not prevail; he gives the keys of the kingdom
Grants the disciples authority to bind and loose — heaven will ratify their decisions
If your brother sins, go privately — if he listens, you have won him; take witnesses; then tell the church; treat the unrepentant as an outsider
Where two or three are gathered in his name, there he is among them; if two agree about anything they ask, it will be done
A kingdom divided against itself cannot stand
